content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: pulp-platform/axi
项目名: axi
讲义目录: axi-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/
当前 HEAD: e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "axi",
  "rationale": "本项目是 pulp-platform 维护的大型 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库（88 个源文件，Bender 依赖分为 Level 0–6），因此学习路线直接对齐其天然的依赖层级：先打基础再向上组合。整体分为三层共 16 个单元约 50 篇讲义。入门层（U1–U3）从项目定位、构建系统（Bender/Makefile/scripts）、AXI4 协议回顾出发，再讲三大基础设施——axi_pkg 类型常量、axi_intf 接口与 typedef/assign/port 宏，最后是 axi_test 验证工具与测试台写法，让读者能「看懂、能跑」。进阶层（U4–U14）按模块类别逐族展开：总线连接原语（join/cut/delayer）、多路复用核心（demux/mux）、全连接交叉开关 xbar、流控缓冲（fifo/isolate/throttle）、时钟域跨越（cdc）、突发与序列化、ID 宽度管理、数据宽度转换、AXI-Lite 子系统、协议转换桥、存储端点适配器。每一族都从最底层模块讲到其组合体，遵循「先零件后整机」。专家层（U15–U16）聚焦原子操作 ATOPs、交叉点 xp/interleaved_xbar、异构网络设计取舍、定时与流水线策略、验证方法学和 CI/EDA 兼容等架构级议题。讲义之间通过 depends_on 显式约束先后，避免读者跳读缺失前置知识。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "项目入门与整体认知",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目定位与设计哲学",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 pulp-platform/axi 是什么：一套用于构建片上通信网络的 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库，以及它的四大设计目标（拓扑无关、模块化组合、适配异构网络、完全协议合规）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解本库解决什么问题、与手写 AXI 互联的区别",
            "掌握拓扑无关、组合优于配置的 Unix 哲学在硬件中的应用",
            "认识 AXI4+ATOPs 的含义及库内模块的整体分类"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/axi_xbar.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README",
            "axi_xbar"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 的模块列表，按「互连」「转换」「端点」「验证」四类给其中 10 个模块归类，并写一段话说明你会用哪些模块拼出一个两主一从的小型互联。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库结构与构建系统",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-repo-and-build.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解仓库目录组织（src/include/test/doc/scripts）以及 Bender 包管理器如何用依赖层级（Level 0–6）定义编译顺序，并介绍 src_files.yml、axi.core、ips_list.yml 等清单文件的作用。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "看懂 Bender.yml 中 sources 的层级化组织及其含义",
            "理解 include/ 下三类 .svh 宏文件如何被导出",
            "掌握 src/、test/、scripts/ 各自承担的职责"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Bender.yml",
            "src_files.yml",
            "axi.core",
            "ips_list.yml"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Bender.yml",
            "src_files.yml"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 Bender.yml 的层级说明 axi_xbar（Level 5）依赖哪些更低层级的文件，并解释为什么 axi_pkg 必须是 Level 0。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "AXI4 协议快速回顾",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "用本库源码视角回顾 AXI4：五个通道（AW/W/B/AR/R）、valid/ready 握手、突发类型与响应码，以及「in flight / pending」等术语，为后续阅读模块打协议基础。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "说出五个 AXI 通道及其数据流方向",
            "理解 valid/ready 握手与 in flight、pending 的精确定义",
            "区分 BURST_FIXED/INCR/WRAP 与 RESP_OKAY/EXOKAY/SLVERR/DECERR"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/axi_pkg.sv",
            "doc/README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg",
            "doc/README"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 axi_pkg.sv 中找到 BURST_* 与 RESP_* 的 localparam 定义，画出一次 4 拍 INCR 写事务在五个通道上的时序草图。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "如何编译、仿真与综合",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-compile-sim-synth.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解如何用 Makefile 与 scripts/ 下的脚本完成编译（compile_vsim.sh）、仿真（run_vsim.sh）、Verilator lint（run_verilator.sh）与综合（synth.sh），以及 test/ 目录的 testbench 命名约定。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 make compile.log / sim-<tb>.log / elab.log 跑通流程",
            "理解 Bender target（rtl/test/simulation/synthesis/synth_test）的作用",
            "知道 test/ 下 tb_*.sv 与对应 DUT 的对应关系"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "Makefile",
            "scripts/compile_vsim.sh",
            "scripts/run_vsim.sh",
            "scripts/synth.sh",
            "scripts/run_verilator.sh"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "Makefile",
            "scripts"
          ],
          "practice_task": "执行 make help 查看可用目标，然后选择一个 testbench（如 tb_axi_lite_regs）跑一次仿真并解释日志中「Errors: 0」的含义。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "基础设施 — 类型、接口与宏",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "axi_pkg：类型与常量",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-axi-pkg-types.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "精读 axi_pkg.sv 的参数化宽度常量（BurstWidth、RespWidth 等）、typedef（burst_t、resp_t、atop_t 等）以及 BURST/RESP/CACHE 等 localparam，这是全库共享的类型根基。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 axi_pkg 中所有公共 typedef 与 localparam 的来源",
            "理解为什么把这些类型集中在一个 package 里",
            "能引用 axi_pkg:: 下的类型来声明自己的信号"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_pkg.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg"
          ],
          "practice_task": "列出 axi_pkg 中每个 typedef 对应的位宽常量，并写一小段 SystemVerilog 用 axi_pkg::resp_t 和 RESP_DECERR 声明并赋值一个信号。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "axi_pkg：辅助函数与配置结构体",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-axi-pkg-funcs-cfg.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_pkg 中的辅助函数（如 num_bytes、地址对齐计算）以及 xbar_cfg_t、xbar_rule_64_t/32_t、xbar_latency_e 等配置结构体，它们是参数化模块（尤其 xbar）的配置入口。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "使用 axi_pkg 的函数完成字节/拍数计算",
            "看懂 xbar_cfg_t 各字段含义并能构造一个配置",
            "理解 xbar_latency_e 枚举（CUT_ALL_AX、NO_LATENCY 等）的用途"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_pkg.sv",
            "doc/axi_xbar.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_pkg",
            "doc/axi_xbar"
          ],
          "practice_task": "对照 doc/axi_xbar.md 的字段表，为「2 个 slave 端口、3 个 master 端口」写出一个 xbar_cfg_t 实例，并选择合适的 LatencyMode。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "axi_intf：SystemVerilog 接口",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-axi-intf.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "精读 axi_intf.sv 中定义的 AXI_BUS、AXI_BUS_DV（带时钟的验证用接口）、AXI_LITE 等接口及其 Master/Slave/Monitor modport，理解接口如何封装五通道信号。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 AXI_BUS 接口的 modport 如何区分主从监听",
            "掌握 AXI_BUS_DV 引入 clk_i 的用途",
            "能在一个模块端口上正确使用 AXI_BUS.Slave"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_intf.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_intf"
          ],
          "practice_task": "基于 AXI_BUS_DV 写一个最小的 monitor 模块，用 Monitor modport 端口把 AW 通道的 id/addr 打印出来。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "typedef / assign / port 宏体系",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-typedef-assign-port-macros.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 include/axi/ 下三大宏文件：typedef.svh（声明通道/请求/响应 struct）、assign.svh（在接口与 struct 间互连）、port.svh（生成扁平 AXI 端口），它们是减少重复样板代码的关键。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 AXI_TYPEDEF_* 宏声明一组通道与 req/resp struct",
            "用 AXI_ASSIGN_* 宏在 AXI_BUS 接口与 struct 之间搬数据",
            "理解 port.svh 宏在顶层扁平化端口时的作用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "include/axi/typedef.svh",
            "include/axi/assign.svh",
            "include/axi/port.svh",
            "src/axi_xbar.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "typedef.svh",
            "assign.svh",
            "port.svh"
          ],
          "practice_task": "仿照 axi_xbar_intf 中的写法，用 AXI_TYPEDEF_* 与 AXI_ASSIGN_* 宏为一个自定义模块声明 req/resp 类型并把它们连到 AXI_BUS 接口。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2",
            "u2-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "仿真与验证基础设施",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "axi_test：底层驱动",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-axi-test-drivers.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_test.sv 中的 axi_lite_driver 与 axi_driver 类：如何用 send_aw/send_w recv_b 等任务在通道上逐拍驱动，以及 TA/TT 时序参数的含义。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "用 driver 类发送单拍握手并理解 application/test time",
            "掌握 reset_master/reset_slave 的初始化套路",
            "理解 driver 与 AXI_BUS_DV 虚接口的绑定方式"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/axi_test.sv"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "axi_test"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用 axi_lite_driver 写一段过程：发起一次 32 位写、再发起一次读并检查返回数据，标注 TA/TT 的作用。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l3",
            "u2-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "随机主从、scoreboard 与 sim_mem",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u3-l2-rand-master-slave-scoreboard.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 axi_test 中的 axi_rand_master/axi_rand_slave/axi_lite_rand_*、axi_scoreboard 以及 axi_sim_mem，它们是构建随机激励与自检测试台的核心组件。",
          "learning_goals": [
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目定位与设计哲学

本讲为手册起点，定位 pulp-platform/axi：一个用 SystemVerilog 编写、遵循 AXI4 / AXI4-Lite 的片上通信网络 IP 库，既给拼搭网络的积木，也自带端点（DMA、存储控制器）与验证组件。核心哲学是「组合优于配置」——把 Unix 哲学搬进硬件，宁可把职责单一的小模块背靠背串起来，也不靠堆参数；axi_xbar 本身就是 unmuxed（demux 阵列）加 axi_mux 阵列拼成，是其活样本。引入术语：Master/Slave 主从、片上互联、五通道 valid/ready 握手、in flight（在途）与 pending（挂起）、AXI4+ATOPs（AXI4 叠加 AXI5 原子操作）。ATOP_R_RESP 位的原子写会同时产生 B 和 R 响应，不支持 ATOP 的从端前须加 axi_atop_filter。模块可按互连 / 转换 / 端点 / 验证四类建立心智地图。关键结论：xbar 的 master 端口 ID 比 slave 端口宽 ⌈log2(NoSlvPorts)⌉ 位，用于路由响应。本讲不要求懂协议细节。
### u1-l2 仓库结构与构建系统

本讲讲解 `pulp-platform/axi` 仓库的组织与构建，不涉及 AXI 协议细节。仓库按职责分目录：`src/` 放可综合 RTL，`include/axi/` 放 `.svh` 宏文件，`test/` 放 `tb_*.sv` 测试台，`doc/` 与 `scripts/` 分别存放文档和构建脚本。核心概念是 **Level 0–6 编译层级**：由于被 `import` 的 `package` 必须先编译，本库把依赖关系做拓扑分层，一个文件的层级等于其在本包内最长依赖链加一，按层级升序即可保证正确编译。其中 `axi_pkg.sv` 作为全库共享类型的 `package`，被几乎所有模块引用且无内部依赖，因此位于 Level 0，是依赖图的根。仓库并行维护 `Bender.yml`、`axi.core`、`src_files.yml` 三份层级清单，仅为兼容 Bender、FuseSoC 与朴素脚本三类工具链；`ips_list.yml` 则是钉死外部依赖 commit 的发布清单。验证专用文件（`axi_test` 等）不可综合，三份清单均将其单独隔离。`include/` 通过 Bender 的 `export_include_dirs` 导出，使下游可直接 `\`include "axi/typedef.svh"`。后续讲义将承接 `axi_pkg` 中的类型定义回顾协议，并拆解 `Makefile` 与脚本的实际用法。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u1-l3
- 文件名: u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md
- 写入路径: axi-tutorial/u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md
- 标题: AXI4 协议快速回顾
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 用本库源码视角回顾 AXI4：五个通道（AW/W/B/AR/R）、valid/ready 握手、突发类型与响应码，以及「in flight / pending」等术语，为后续阅读模块打协议基础。
- 学习目标: 说出五个 AXI 通道及其数据流方向；理解 valid/ready 握手与 in flight、pending 的精确定义；区分 BURST_FIXED/INCR/WRAP 与 RESP_OKAY/EXOKAY/SLVERR/DECERR
- 应覆盖的最小模块: axi_pkg，doc/README
- 关键源码: README.md，src/axi_pkg.sv，doc/README.md
- 代码实践任务: 在 axi_pkg.sv 中找到 BURST_* 与 RESP_* 的 localparam 定义，画出一次 4 拍 INCR 写事务在五个通道上的时序草图。
- 依赖讲义: u1-l1

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `axi-tutorial/u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: pulp-platform/axi
项目名: axi
讲义目录: axi-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/blob/e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407/
当前 HEAD: e55ae2a7ee606ee3cfd4257f63982a971b704407
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
  "project": "axi",
  "rationale": "本项目是 pulp-platform 维护的大型 SystemVerilog AXI4/AXI4-Lite IP 库（88 个源文件，Bender 依赖分为 Level 0–6），因此学习路线直接对齐其天然的依赖层级：先打基础再向上组合。整体分为三层共 16 个单元约 50 篇讲义。入门层（U1–U3）从项目定位、构建系统（Bender/Makefile/scripts）、AXI4 协议回顾出发，再讲三大基础设施——axi_pkg 类型常量、axi_intf 接口与 typedef/assign/port 宏，最后是 axi_test 验证工具与测试台写法，让读者能「看懂、能跑」。进阶层（U4–U14）按模块类别逐族展开：总线连接原语（join/cut/delayer）、多路复用核心（demux/mux）、全连接交叉开关 xbar、流控缓冲（fifo/isolate/throttle）、时钟域跨越（cdc）、突发与序列化、ID 宽度管理、数据宽度转换、AXI-Lite 子系统、协议转换桥、存储端点适配器。每一族都从最底层模块讲到其组合体，遵循「先零件后整机」。专家层（U15–U16）聚焦原子操作 ATOPs、交叉点 xp/interleaved_xbar、异构网络设计取舍、定时与流水线策略、验证方法学和 CI/EDA 兼容等架构级议题。讲义之间通过 depends_on 显式约束先后，避免读者跳读缺失前置知识。",
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目定位与设计哲学

本讲为手册起点，定位 pulp-platform/axi：一个用 SystemVerilog 编写、遵循 AXI4 / AXI4-Lite 的片上通信网络 IP 库，既给拼搭网络的积木，也自带端点（DMA、存储控制器）与验证组件。核心哲学是「组合优于配置」——把 Unix 哲学搬进硬件，宁可把职责单一的小模块背靠背串起来，也不靠堆参数；axi_xbar 本身就是 unmuxed（demux 阵列）加 axi_mux 阵列拼成，是其活样本。引入术语：Master/Slave 主从、片上互联、五通道 valid/ready 握手、in flight（在途）与 pending（挂起）、AXI4+ATOPs（AXI4 叠加 AXI5 原子操作）。ATOP_R_RESP 位的原子写会同时产生 B 和 R 响应，不支持 ATOP 的从端前须加 axi_atop_filter。模块可按互连 / 转换 / 端点 / 验证四类建立心智地图。关键结论：xbar 的 master 端口 ID 比 slave 端口宽 ⌈log2(NoSlvPorts)⌉ 位，用于路由响应。本讲不要求懂协议细节。
### u1-l2 仓库结构与构建系统

本讲讲解 `pulp-platform/axi` 仓库的组织与构建，不涉及 AXI 协议细节。仓库按职责分目录：`src/` 放可综合 RTL，`include/axi/` 放 `.svh` 宏文件，`test/` 放 `tb_*.sv` 测试台，`doc/` 与 `scripts/` 分别存放文档和构建脚本。核心概念是 **Level 0–6 编译层级**：由于被 `import` 的 `package` 必须先编译，本库把依赖关系做拓扑分层，一个文件的层级等于其在本包内最长依赖链加一，按层级升序即可保证正确编译。其中 `axi_pkg.sv` 作为全库共享类型的 `package`，被几乎所有模块引用且无内部依赖，因此位于 Level 0，是依赖图的根。仓库并行维护 `Bender.yml`、`axi.core`、`src_files.yml` 三份层级清单，仅为兼容 Bender、FuseSoC 与朴素脚本三类工具链；`ips_list.yml` 则是钉死外部依赖 commit 的发布清单。验证专用文件（`axi_test` 等）不可综合，三份清单均将其单独隔离。`include/` 通过 Bender 的 `export_include_dirs` 导出，使下游可直接 `\`include "axi/typedef.svh"`。后续讲义将承接 `axi_pkg` 中的类型定义回顾协议，并拆解 `Makefile` 与脚本的实际用法。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u1-l3
- 文件名: u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md
- 写入路径: axi-tutorial/u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md
- 标题: AXI4 协议快速回顾
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 用本库源码视角回顾 AXI4：五个通道（AW/W/B/AR/R）、valid/ready 握手、突发类型与响应码，以及「in flight / pending」等术语，为后续阅读模块打协议基础。
- 学习目标: 说出五个 AXI 通道及其数据流方向；理解 valid/ready 握手与 in flight、pending 的精确定义；区分 BURST_FIXED/INCR/WRAP 与 RESP_OKAY/EXOKAY/SLVERR/DECERR
- 应覆盖的最小模块: axi_pkg，doc/README
- 关键源码: README.md，src/axi_pkg.sv，doc/README.md
- 代码实践任务: 在 axi_pkg.sv 中找到 BURST_* 与 RESP_* 的 localparam 定义，画出一次 4 拍 INCR 写事务在五个通道上的时序草图。
- 依赖讲义: u1-l1

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `axi-tutorial/u1-l3-axi-protocol-primer.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

23	
24	On a channel with valid and ready signals, a *handshake* occurs when both valid and ready are high on a clock edge.  See section A3.2.1 of the AXI Spec for details on the handshake process.
25	
26	### In Flight
27

file.content:
# Documentation of AXI Modules

This folder contains the documentation of the following modules:

- [AXI Crossbar (`axi_xbar`)](axi_xbar.md)
- [AXI Demultiplexer (`axi_demux`)](axi_demux.md)
- [AXI Multiplexer (`axi_mux`)](axi_mux.md)
- [AXI4-Lite Mailbox (`axi_lite_mailbox`)](axi_lite_mailbox.md)


## Relevant Specifications

We adhere to the *AMBA AXI and ACE Protocol Specification, Issue F.b*, abbreviated as *AXI Spec* in these documents.


## Terminology

We follow the terminology defined in the AXI Glossary (see p. 433 of the AXI Spec).

Additionally, we define the following terms.

### Handshake

On a channel with valid and ready signals, a *handshake* occurs when both valid and ready are high on a clock edge.  See section A3.2.1 of the AXI Spec for details on the handshake process.

### In Flight

A transaction is *in flight* for a given interface when the handshake of the `Ax` beat of a transaction has occurred on that interface but the handshake of the (last) response of that transaction has not yet occurred on that interface.

### Pending

A handshake is *pending* for a given channel when the valid signal is high and the ready signal is low.

21	//! AXI Package
22	/// Contains all necessary type definitions, constants, and generally useful functions.
23	package axi_pkg;
24	    /// AXI Transaction Burst Width.
25	  parameter int unsigned BurstWidth  = 32'd2;
26	  /// AXI Transaction Response Width.
27	  parameter int unsigned RespWidth   = 32'd2;
28	  /// AXI Transaction Cacheability Width.
29	  parameter int unsigned CacheWidth  = 32'd4;
30	  /// AXI Transaction Protection Width.
31	  parameter int unsigned ProtWidth   = 32'd3;
32	  /// AXI Transaction Quality of Service Width.
33	  parameter int unsigned QosWidth    = 32'd4;
34	  /// AXI Transaction Region Width.
35	  parameter int unsigned RegionWidth = 32'd4;
36	  /// AXI Transaction Length Width.
37	  parameter int unsigned LenWidth    = 32'd8;
38	  /// AXI Transaction Size Width.
39	  parameter int unsigned SizeWidth   = 32'd3;
40	  /// AXI Lock Width.
41	  parameter int unsigned LockWidth   = 32'd1;
42	  /// AXI5 Atomic Operation Width.
43	  parameter int unsigned AtopWidth   = 32'd6;
44	  /// AXI5 Non-Secure Address Identifier.
45	  parameter int unsigned NsaidWidth  = 32'd4;
46	
47	  /// AXI Transaction Burst Width.
48	  typedef logic [1:0]  burst_t;
49	  /// AXI Transaction Response Type.
50	  typedef logic [1:0]   resp_t;
51	  /// AXI Transaction Cacheability Type.
52	  typedef logic [3:0]  cache_t;
53	  /// AXI Transaction Protection Type.
54	  typedef logic [2:0]   prot_t;
55	  /// AXI Transaction Quality of Service Type.
56	  typedef logic [3:0]    qos_t;
57	  /// AXI Transaction Region Type.
58	  typedef logic [3:0] region_t;
59	  /// AXI Transaction Length Type.
60	  typedef logic [7:0]    len_t;
61	  /// AXI Transaction Size Type.
62	  typedef logic [2:0]   size_t;
63	  /// AXI5 Atomic Operation Type.
64	  typedef logic [5:0]   atop_t; // atomic operations
65	  /// AXI5 Non-Secure Address Identifier.
66	  typedef logic [3:0]  nsaid_t;
67	
68	  /// In a fixed burst:
69	  /// - The address is the same for every transfer in the burst.
70	  /// - The byte lanes that are valid are constant for all beats in the burst.  However, within
71	  ///   those byte lanes, the actual bytes that have `wstrb` asserted can differ for each beat in
72	  ///   the burst.
73	  /// This burst type is used for repeated accesses to the same location such as when loading or
74	  /// emptying a FIFO.
75	  localparam BURST_FIXED = 2'b00;
76	  /// In an incrementing burst, the address for each transfer in the burst is an increment of the
77	  /// address for the previous transfer.  The increment value depends on the size of the transfer.
78	  /// For example, the address for each transfer in a burst with a size of 4 bytes is the previous
79	  /// address plus four.
80	  /// This burst type is used for accesses to normal sequential memory.
81	  localparam BURST_INCR  = 2'b01;
82	  /// A wrapping burst is similar to an incrementing burst, except that the address wraps around to
83	  /// a lower address if an upper address limit is reached.
84	  /// The following restrictions apply to wrapping bursts:
85	  /// - The start address must be aligned to the size of each transfer.
86	  /// - The length of the burst must be 2, 4, 8, or 16 transfers.
87	  localparam BURST_WRAP  = 2'b10;
88	
89	  /// Normal access success.  Indicates that a normal access has been successful. Can also indicate
90	  /// that an exclusive access has failed.
91	  localparam RESP_OKAY   = 2'b00;
92	  /// Exclusive access okay.  Indicates that either the read or write portion of an exclusive access
93	  /// has been successful.
94	  localparam RESP_EXOKAY = 2'b01;
95	  /// Slave error.  Used when the access has reached the slave successfully, but the slave wishes to
96	  /// return an error condition to the originating master.
97	  localparam RESP_SLVERR = 2'b10;
98	  /// Decode error.  Generated, typically by an interconnect component, to indicate that there is no
99	  /// slave at the transaction address.
100	  localparam RESP_DECERR = 2'b11;
101	
102	  /// When this bit is asserted, the interconnect, or any component, can delay the transaction
103	  /// reaching its final destination for any number of cycles.
104	  localparam CACHE_BUFFERABLE = 4'b0001;
105	  /// When HIGH, Modifiable indicates that the characteristics of the transaction can be modified.
106	  /// When Modifiable is LOW, the transaction is Non-modifiable.
107	  localparam CACHE_MODIFIABLE = 4'b0010;
108	  /// When this bit is asserted, read allocation of the transaction is recommended but is not
109	  /// mandatory.
110	  localparam CACHE_RD_ALLOC   = 4'b0100;
111	  /// When this bit is asserted, write allocation of the transaction is recommended but is not
112	  /// mandatory.
113	  localparam CACHE_WR_ALLOC   = 4'b1000;
114	
115	  /// Maximum number of bytes per burst, as specified by `size` (see Table A3-2).
116	  function automatic shortint unsigned num_bytes(size_t size);
117	    return shortint'(1 << size);
118	  endfunction
119	
120	  /// An overly long address type.
121	  /// It lets us define functions that work generically for shorter addresses.  We rely on the
122	  /// synthesizer to optimize the unused bits away.
123	  typedef logic [127:0] largest_addr_t;
124	
125	  /// Aligned address of burst (see A3-51).
126	  function automatic largest_addr_t aligned_addr(largest_addr_t addr, size_t size);
127	    return (addr >> size) << size;
128	  endfunction
129	
130	  /// Warp boundary of a `BURST_WRAP` transfer (see A3-51).
131	  /// This is the lowest address accessed within a wrapping burst.
132	  /// This address is aligned to the size and length of the burst.
133	  /// The length of a `BURST_WRAP` has to be 2, 4, 8, or 16 transfers.
134	  function automatic largest_addr_t wrap_boundary (largest_addr_t addr, size_t size, len_t len);
135	    largest_addr_t wrap_addr;
136	
137	    // pragma translate_off
138	    `ifndef VERILATOR
139	      assume (len == len_t'(4'b1) || len == len_t'(4'b11) || len == len_t'(4'b111) ||
140	          len == len_t'(4'b1111)) else
141	        $error("AXI BURST_WRAP with not allowed len of: %0h", len);
142	    `endif
143	    // pragma translate_on
144	
145	    // In A3-51 the wrap boundary is defined as:
146	    // `Wrap_Boundary = (INT(Start_Address / (Number_Bytes × Burst_Length))) ×
147	    //    (Number_Bytes × Burst_Length)`
148	    // Whereas the aligned address is defined as:
149	    // `Aligned_Address = (INT(Start_Address / Number_Bytes)) × Number_Bytes`
150	    // This leads to the wrap boundary using the same calculation as the aligned address, difference
151	    // being the additional dependency on the burst length. The addition in the case statement
152	    // is equal to the multiplication with `Burst_Length` as a shift (used by `aligned_addr`) is
153	    // equivalent with multiplication and division by a power of two, which conveniently are the
154	    // only allowed values for `len` of a `BURST_WRAP`.
155	    unique case (len)
156	      len_t'(4'b1   ) : wrap_addr = (addr >> (unsigned'(size) + 1)) << (unsigned'(size) + 1); // multiply `Number_Bytes` by `2`
157	      len_t'(4'b11  ) : wrap_addr = (addr >> (unsigned'(size) + 2)) << (unsigned'(size) + 2); // multiply `Number_Bytes` by `4`
158	      len_t'(4'b111 ) : wrap_addr = (addr >> (unsigned'(size) + 3)) << (unsigned'(size) + 3); // multiply `Number_Bytes` by `8`
159	      len_t'(4'b1111) : wrap_addr = (addr >> (unsigned'(size) + 4)) << (unsigned'(size) + 4); // multiply `Number_Bytes` by `16`
160	      default : wrap_addr = '0;
161	    endcase
162	    return wrap_addr;
163	  endfunction
164	
165	  /// Address of beat (see A3-51).
166	  function automatic largest_addr_t
167	  beat_addr(largest_addr_t addr, size_t size, len_t len, burst_t burst, shortint unsigned i_beat);
168	    largest_addr_t ret_addr = addr;
169	    largest_addr_t wrp_bond = '0;
170	    if (burst == BURST_WRAP) begin
171	      // do not trigger the function if there is no wrapping burst, to prevent assumptions firing
172	      wrp_bond = wrap_boundary(addr, size, len);
173	    end
174	    if (i_beat != 0 && burst != BURST_FIXED) begin
175	      // From A3-51:
176	      // For an INCR burst, and for a WRAP burst for which the address has not wrapped, this
177	      // equation determines the address of any transfer after the first transfer in a burst:
178	      // `Address_N = Aligned_Address + (N – 1) × Number_Bytes` (N counts from 1 to len!)
179	      ret_addr = aligned_addr(addr, size) + i_beat * num_bytes(size);
180	      // From A3-51:
181	      // For a WRAP burst, if Address_N = Wrap_Boundary + (Number_Bytes × Burst_Length), then:
182	      // * Use this equation for the current transfer:
183	      //     `Address_N = Wrap_Boundary`
184	      // * Use this equation for any subsequent transfers:
185	      //     `Address_N = Start_Address + ((N – 1) × Number_Bytes) – (Number_Bytes × Burst_Length)`
186	      // This means that the address calculation of a `BURST_WRAP` fundamentally works the same
187	      // as for a `BURST_INC`, the difference is when the calculated address increments
188	      // over the wrap threshold, the address wraps around by subtracting the accessed address
189	      // space from the normal `BURST_INCR` address. The lower wrap boundary is equivalent to
190	      // The wrap trigger condition minus the container size (`num_bytes(size) * (len + 1)`).
191	      if (burst == BURST_WRAP && ret_addr >= wrp_bond + (num_bytes(size) * (largest_addr_t'(len) + 1))) begin
192	        ret_addr = ret_addr - (num_bytes(size) * (largest_addr_t'(len) + 1));
193	      end
194	    end
195	    return ret_addr;
196	  endfunction
197	
198	  /// Index of lowest byte in beat (see A3-51).
199	  function automatic shortint unsigned
200	  beat_lower_byte(largest_addr_t addr, size_t size, len_t len, burst_t burst,
201	      shortint unsigned strobe_width, shortint unsigned i_beat);
202	    largest_addr_t _addr = beat_addr(addr, size, len, burst, i_beat);
203	    return shortint'(($bits(_addr)  + $bits(strobe_width))'(_addr) - (_addr / largest_addr_t'(strobe_width)) * strobe_width);
204	  endfunction
205	
206	  /// Index of highest byte in beat (see A3-51).
207	  function automatic shortint unsigned
208	  beat_upper_byte(largest_addr_t addr, size_t size, len_t len, burst_t burst,
209	      shortint unsigned strobe_width, shortint unsigned i_beat);
210	      typedef shortint unsigned SU;
211	    if (i_beat == 0) begin
212	      return SU'(aligned_addr(addr, size) + (largest_addr_t'(num_bytes(size)) - 1) - (addr / largest_addr_t'(strobe_width)) * strobe_width);
213	    end else begin
214	      return beat_lower_byte(addr, size, len, burst, strobe_width, i_beat) + num_bytes(size) - 1;
215	    end
216	  endfunction
217	
218	  /// Is the bufferable bit set?
219	  function automatic logic bufferable(cache_t cache);
220	    return |(cache & CACHE_BUFFERABLE);
221	  endfunction
222	
223	  /// Is the modifiable bit set?
224	  function automatic logic modifiable(cache_t cache);
225	    return |(cache & CACHE_MODIFIABLE);
226	  endfunction
227	
228	  /// Memory Type.
229	  typedef enum logic [3:0] {
230	    DEVICE_NONBUFFERABLE,
231	    DEVICE_BUFFERABLE,
232	    NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_NONBUFFERABLE,
233	    NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE,
234	    WTHRU_NOALLOCATE,
235	    WTHRU_RALLOCATE,
236	    WTHRU_WALLOCATE,
237	    WTHRU_RWALLOCATE,
238	    WBACK_NOALLOCATE,
239	    WBACK_RALLOCATE,
240	    WBACK_WALLOCATE,
241	    WBACK_RWALLOCATE
242	  } mem_type_t;
243	
244	  /// Create an `AR_CACHE` field from a `mem_type_t` type.
245	  function automatic logic [3:0] get_arcache(mem_type_t mtype);
246	    unique case (mtype)
247	      DEVICE_NONBUFFERABLE              : return 4'b0000;
248	      DEVICE_BUFFERABLE                 : return 4'b0001;
249	      NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_NONBUFFERABLE : return 4'b0010;
250	      NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE    : return 4'b0011;
251	      WTHRU_NOALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1010;
252	      WTHRU_RALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1110;
253	      WTHRU_WALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1010;
254	      WTHRU_RWALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1110;
255	      WBACK_NOALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1011;
256	      WBACK_RALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1111;
257	      WBACK_WALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1011;
258	      WBACK_RWALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1111;
259	      default                           : return 4'bxxxx;
260	    endcase // mtype
261	  endfunction
262	
263	  /// Create an `AW_CACHE` field from a `mem_type_t` type.
264	  function automatic logic [3:0] get_awcache(mem_type_t mtype);
265	    unique case (mtype)
266	      DEVICE_NONBUFFERABLE              : return 4'b0000;
267	      DEVICE_BUFFERABLE                 : return 4'b0001;
268	      NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_NONBUFFERABLE : return 4'b0010;
269	      NORMAL_NONCACHEABLE_BUFFERABLE    : return 4'b0011;
270	      WTHRU_NOALLOCATE                  : return 4'b0110;
271	      WTHRU_RALLOCATE                   : return 4'b0110;
272	      WTHRU_WALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1110;
273	      WTHRU_RWALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1110;
274	      WBACK_NOALLOCATE                  : return 4'b0111;
275	      WBACK_RALLOCATE                   : return 4'b0111;
276	      WBACK_WALLOCATE                   : return 4'b1111;
277	      WBACK_RWALLOCATE                  : return 4'b1111;
278	      default                           : return 4'bxxxx;
279	    endcase // mtype
280	  endfunction
281	
282	  /// RESP precedence: DECERR > SLVERR > OKAY > EXOKAY.  This is not defined in the AXI standard but
283	  /// depends on the implementation.  We consistently use the precedence above.  Rationale:
284	  /// - EXOKAY means an exclusive access was successful, whereas OKAY means it was not.  Thus, if
285	  ///   OKAY and EXOKAY are to be merged, OKAY precedes because the exclusive access was not fully
286	  ///   successful.
287	  /// - Both DECERR and SLVERR mean (part of) a transaction were unsuccessful, whereas OKAY means an
288	  ///   entire transaction was successful.  Thus both DECERR and SLVERR precede OKAY.
289	  /// - DECERR means (part of) a transactions could not be routed to a slave component, whereas
290	  ///   SLVERR means the transaction reached a slave component but lead to an error condition there.
291	  ///   Thus DECERR precedes SLVERR because DECERR happens earlier in the handling of a transaction.
292	  function automatic resp_t resp_precedence(resp_t resp_a, resp_t resp_b);
293	    unique case (resp_a)
294	      RESP_OKAY: begin
295	        // Any response except EXOKAY precedes OKAY.
296	        if (resp_b == RESP_EXOKAY) begin
297	          return resp_a;
298	        end else begin
299	          return resp_b;
300	        end
301	      end
302	      RESP_EXOKAY: begin
303	        // Any response precedes EXOKAY.
304	        return resp_b;
305	      end
306	      RESP_SLVERR: begin
307	        // Only DECERR precedes SLVERR.
308	        if (resp_b == RESP_DECERR) begin
309	          return resp_b;
310	        end else begin
311	          return resp_a;
312	        end
313	      end
314	      RESP_DECERR: begin
315	        // No response precedes DECERR.
316	        return resp_a;
317	      end
318	    endcase
319	  endfunction
320	
321	  /// AW Width: Returns the width of the AW channel payload
322	  function automatic int unsigned aw_width(int unsigned addr_width, int unsigned id_width,
323	                                           int unsigned user_width );
324	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals
325	    return (id_width + addr_width + LenWidth + SizeWidth + BurstWidth + LockWidth + CacheWidth +
326	            ProtWidth + QosWidth + RegionWidth + AtopWidth + user_width );
327	  endfunction
328	
329	  /// W Width: Returns the width of the W channel payload
330	  function automatic int unsigned w_width(int unsigned data_width, int unsigned user_width );
331	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals
332	    return (data_width + data_width / 32'd8 + 32'd1 + user_width);
333	    //                   ^- StrobeWidth       ^- LastWidth
334	  endfunction
335	
336	  /// B Width: Returns the width of the B channel payload
337	  function automatic int unsigned b_width(int unsigned id_width, int unsigned user_width );
338	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals
339	    return (id_width + RespWidth + user_width);
340	  endfunction
341	
342	  /// AR Width: Returns the width of the AR channel payload
343	  function automatic int unsigned ar_width(int unsigned addr_width, int unsigned id_width,
344	                                           int unsigned user_width );
345	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals
346	    return (id_width + addr_width + LenWidth + SizeWidth + BurstWidth + LockWidth + CacheWidth +
347	            ProtWidth + QosWidth + RegionWidth + user_width );
348	  endfunction
349	
350	  /// R Width: Returns the width of the R channel payload
351	  function automatic int unsigned r_width(int unsigned data_width, int unsigned id_width,
352	                                          int unsigned user_width );
353	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals
354	    return (id_width + data_width + RespWidth + 32'd1 + user_width);
355	    //                                          ^- LastWidth
356	  endfunction
357	
358	  /// Request Width: Returns the width of the request channel
359	  function automatic int unsigned req_width(int unsigned addr_width,    int unsigned data_width,
360	                                            int unsigned id_width,      int unsigned aw_user_width,
361	                                            int unsigned ar_user_width, int unsigned w_user_width   );
362	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals and their handshakes
363	    //                                                      v- valids
364	    return (aw_width(addr_width, id_width, aw_user_width) + 32'd1 +
365	            w_width(data_width, w_user_width)             + 32'd1 +
366	            ar_width(addr_width, id_width, ar_user_width) + 32'd1 + 32'd1 + 32'd1 );
367	    //                                                              ^- R,   ^- B ready
368	  endfunction
369	
370	  /// Response Width: Returns the width of the response channel
371	  function automatic int unsigned rsp_width(int unsigned data_width,   int unsigned id_width,
372	                                            int unsigned r_user_width, int unsigned b_user_width );
373	    // Sum the individual bit widths of the signals and their handshakes
374	    //                                                    v- valids
375	    return (r_width(data_width, id_width, r_user_width) + 32'd1 +
376	            b_width(id_width, b_user_width)             + 32'd1 + 32'd1 + 32'd1 + 32'd1);
377	    //                                                            ^- AW,  ^- AR,  ^- W ready
378	  endfunction
379	
380	  // ATOP[5:0]
381	  /// - Sends a single data value with an address.
382	  /// - The target swaps the value at the addressed location with the data value that is supplied in
383	  ///   the transaction.
384	  /// - The original data value at the addressed location is returned.
385	  /// - Outbound data size is 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes.
386	  /// - Inbound data size is the same as the outbound data size.
387	  localparam ATOP_ATOMICSWAP  = 6'b110000;
388	  /// - Sends two data values, the compare value and the swap value, to the addressed location.
389	  ///   The compare and swap values are of equal size.
390	  /// - The data value at the addressed location is checked against the compare value:
391	  ///   - If the values match, the swap value is written to the addressed location.
392	  ///   - If the values do not match, the swap value is not written to the addressed location.
393	  /// - The original data value at the addressed location is returned.
394	  /// - Outbound data size is 2, 4, 8, 16, or 32 bytes.
395	  /// - Inbound data size is half of the outbound data size because the outbound data contains both
396	  ///   compare and swap values, whereas the inbound data has only the original data value.
397	  localparam ATOP_ATOMICCMP   = 6'b110001;
398	  // ATOP[5:4]
399	  /// Perform no atomic operation.
400	  localparam ATOP_NONE        = 2'b00;
401	  /// - Sends a single data value with an address and the atomic operation to be performed.
402	  /// - The target performs the operation using the sent data and value at the addressed location as
403	  ///   operands.
404	  /// - The result is stored in the address location.
405	  /// - A single response is given without data.
406	  /// - Outbound data size is 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes.
407	  localparam ATOP_ATOMICSTORE = 2'b01;
408	  /// Sends a single data value with an address and the atomic operation to be performed.
409	  /// - The original data value at the addressed location is returned.
410	  /// - The target performs the operation using the sent data and value at the addressed location as
411	  ///   operands.
412	  /// - The result is stored in the address location.
413	  /// - Outbound data size is 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes.
414	  /// - Inbound data size is the same as the outbound data size.
415	  localparam ATOP_ATOMICLOAD  = 2'b10;
416	  // ATOP[3]
417	  /// For AtomicStore and AtomicLoad transactions `AWATOP[3]` indicates the endianness that is
418	  /// required for the atomic operation.  The value of `AWATOP[3]` applies to arithmetic operations
419	  /// only and is ignored for bitwise logical operations.
420	  /// When deasserted, this bit indicates that the operation is little-endian.
421	  localparam ATOP_LITTLE_END  = 1'b0;
422	  /// When asserted, this bit indicates that the operation is big-endian.
423	  localparam ATOP_BIG_END     = 1'b1;
424	  // ATOP[2:0]
425	  /// The value in memory is added to the sent data and the result stored in memory.
426	  localparam ATOP_ADD   = 3'b000;
427	  /// Every set bit in the sent data clears the corresponding bit of the data in memory.
428	  localparam ATOP_CLR   = 3'b001;
429	  /// Bitwise exclusive OR of the sent data and value in memory.
430	  localparam ATOP_EOR   = 3'b010;
431	  /// Every set bit in the sent data sets the corresponding bit of the data in memory.
432	  localparam ATOP_SET   = 3'b011;
433	  /// The value stored in memory is the maximum of the existing value and sent data. This operation
434	  /// assumes signed data.
435	  localparam ATOP_SMAX  = 3'b100;
436	  /// The value stored in memory is the minimum of the existing value and sent data. This operation
437	  /// assumes signed data.
438	  localparam ATOP_SMIN  = 3'b101;
439	  /// The value stored in memory is the maximum of the existing value and sent data. This operation
440	  /// assumes unsigned data.
441	  localparam ATOP_UMAX  = 3'b110;
442	  /// The value stored in memory is the minimum of the existing value and sent data. This operation
443	  /// assumes unsigned data.
444	  localparam ATOP_UMIN  = 3'b111;
445	  // ATOP[5] == 1'b1 indicated that an atomic transaction has a read response
446	  // Ussage eg: if (req_i.aw.atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]) begin
447	  localparam ATOP_R_RESP = 32'd5;
448	
449	  // `xbar_latency_e` and `xbar_cfg_t` are documented in `doc/axi_xbar.md`.
450	  /// Slice on Demux AW channel.
451	  localparam bit [9:0] DemuxAw = (1 << 9);
452	  /// Slice on Demux W channel.
453	  localparam bit [9:0] DemuxW  = (1 << 8);
454	  /// Slice on Demux B channel.
455	  localparam bit [9:0] DemuxB  = (1 << 7);
456	  /// Slice on Demux AR channel.
457	  localparam bit [9:0] DemuxAr = (1 << 6);
458	  /// Slice on Demux R channel.
459	  localparam bit [9:0] DemuxR  = (1 << 5);
460	  /// Slice on Mux AW channel.
461	  localparam bit [9:0] MuxAw   = (1 << 4);
462	  /// Slice on Mux W channel.
463	  localparam bit [9:0] MuxW    = (1 << 3);
464	  /// Slice on Mux B channel.
465	  localparam bit [9:0] MuxB    = (1 << 2);
466	  /// Slice on Mux AR channel.
467	  localparam bit [9:0] MuxAr   = (1 << 1);
468	  /// Slice on Mux R channel.
469	  localparam bit [9:0] MuxR    = (1 << 0);
470	  /// Latency configuration for `axi_xbar`.
471	  typedef enum bit [9:0] {
472	    NO_LATENCY    = 10'b000_00_000_00,
473	    CUT_SLV_AX    = DemuxAw | DemuxAr,
474	    CUT_MST_AX    = MuxAw | MuxAr,
475	    CUT_ALL_AX    = DemuxAw | DemuxAr | MuxAw | MuxAr,
476	    CUT_SLV_PORTS = DemuxAw | DemuxW | DemuxB | DemuxAr | DemuxR,
477	    CUT_MST_PORTS = MuxAw | MuxW | MuxB | MuxAr | MuxR,
478	    CUT_ALL_PORTS = 10'b111_11_111_11
479	  } xbar_latency_e;
480	
481	  /// Configuration for `axi_xbar`.
482	  typedef struct packed {
483	    /// Number of slave ports of the crossbar.
484	    /// This many master modules are connected to it.
485	    int unsigned   NoSlvPorts;
486	    /// Number of master ports of the crossbar.
487	    /// This many slave modules are connected to it.
488	    int unsigned   NoMstPorts;
489	    /// Maximum number of open transactions each master connected to the crossbar can have in
490	    /// flight at the same time.
491	    int unsigned   MaxMstTrans;
492	    /// Maximum number of open transactions each slave connected to the crossbar can have in
493	    /// flight at the same time.
494	    int unsigned   MaxSlvTrans;
495	    /// Determine if the internal FIFOs of the crossbar are instantiated in fallthrough mode.
496	    /// 0: No fallthrough
497	    /// 1: Fallthrough
498	    bit            FallThrough;
499	    /// The Latency mode of the xbar. This determines if the channels on the ports have
500	    /// a spill register instantiated.
501	    /// Example configurations are provided with the enum `xbar_latency_e`.
502	    bit [9:0]      LatencyMode;
503	    /// This is the number of `axi_multicut` stages instantiated in the line cross of the channels.
504	    /// Having multiple stages can potentially add a large number of FFs!
505	    int unsigned   PipelineStages;
506	    /// AXI ID width of the salve ports. The ID width of the master ports is determined
507	    /// Automatically. See `axi_mux` for details.
508	    int unsigned   AxiIdWidthSlvPorts;
509	    /// The used ID portion to determine if a different salve is used for the same ID.
510	    /// See `axi_demux` for details.
511	    int unsigned   AxiIdUsedSlvPorts;
512	    /// Are IDs unique?
513	    bit            UniqueIds;
514	    /// AXI4+ATOP address field width.
515	    int unsigned   AxiAddrWidth;
516	    /// AXI4+ATOP data field width.
517	    int unsigned   AxiDataWidth;
518	    /// The number of address rules defined for routing of the transactions.
519	    /// Each master port can have multiple rules, should have however at least one.
520	    /// If a transaction can not be routed the xbar will answer with an `axi_pkg::RESP_DECERR`.
521	    int unsigned   NoAddrRules;
522	  } xbar_cfg_t;

524	  /// Commonly used rule types for `axi_xbar` (64-bit addresses).
525	  typedef struct packed {
526	    int unsigned idx;
527	    logic [63:0] start_addr;
528	    logic [63:0] end_addr;
529	  } xbar_rule_64_t;
530	
531	  /// Commonly used rule types for `axi_xbar` (32-bit addresses).
532	  typedef struct packed {
533	    int unsigned idx;
534	    logic [31:0] start_addr;
535	    logic [31:0] end_addr;
536	  } xbar_rule_32_t;
537	
538	  // Return either the argument minus 1 or 0 if 0; useful for IO vector width declaration
539	  function automatic integer unsigned iomsb (input integer unsigned width);
540	      return (width != 32'd0) ? unsigned'(width-1) : 32'd0;
541	  endfunction
542	
543	endpackage
544

content.content:
1	# AXI SystemVerilog Modules for High-Performance On-Chip Communication
2	[![CI status](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi/actions/workflows/gitlab-ci.yml?query=branch%3Amaster)
3	[![GitHub tag (latest SemVer)](https://img.shields.io/github/v/tag/pulp-platform/axi?color=blue&label=current&sort=semver)](CHANGELOG.md)
4	[![SHL-0.51 license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-SHL--0.51-green)](LICENSE)
5	
6	This repository provides modules to build on-chip communication networks adhering to the [AXI4 or AXI4-Lite standards][AMBA 5 Spec].  For high-performance communication, we implement AXI4[+ATOPs from AXI5](#atomic-operations).  For lightweight communication, we implement AXI4-Lite.  We aim to provide a complete end-to-end communication platform, including endpoints such as DMA engines and on-chip memory controllers.
7	
8	Our **design goals** are:
9	- **Topology Independence**: We provide elementary building blocks such as protocol [multiplexers](src/axi_mux.sv) and [demultiplexers](src/axi_demux.sv) that allow users to implement any network topology.  We also provide commonly used interconnecting components such as a [crossbar](src/axi_xbar.sv).
10	- **Modularity**: We favor design by composition over design by configuration where possible.  We strive to apply the *Unix philosophy* to hardware: make each module do one thing well.  This means you will more often instantiate our modules back-to-back than change a parameter value to build more specialized networks.
11	- **Fit for Heterogeneous Networks**: Our modules are parametrizable in terms of data width and transaction concurrency.  This allows to create optimized networks for a wide range of performance (e.g., bandwidth, concurrency, timing), power, and area requirements.  We provide modules such as [data width converters](src/axi_dw_converter.sv) and [ID width converters](src/axi_iw_converter.sv) that allow to join subnetworks with different properties, creating heterogeneous on-chip networks.
12	- **Full AXI Standard Compliance**.
13	- **Compatibility** with a [wide range of (recent versions of) EDA tools](#which-eda-tools-are-supported) and implementation in standardized synthesizable SystemVerilog.
14	
15	The **design and microarchitecture** of the modules in this repository is described in [**this paper**](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9522037) ([preprint](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2009.05334)).  If you use our work in your research, please cite it.
16	
17	
18	## List of Modules
19	
20	In addition to the documents linked in the following table, we are setting up [documentation auto-generated from inline docstrings](https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master).  (Replace `master` in that URL with a tag to get the documentation for a specific version.)
21	
22	| Name                                                        | Description                                                                                          | Doc                              |
23	|-------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|
24	| [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv)                 | Filters atomic operations (ATOPs), i.e., write transactions that have a non-zero `aw_atop` value.    |                                  |
25	| [`axi_burst_splitter`](src/axi_burst_splitter.sv)           | Split AXI4 burst transfers into single-beat transactions.                                            |                                  |
26	| [`axi_burst_splitter_gran`](src/axi_burst_splitter_gran.sv) | Split AXI4 burst transfers into transactions of runtime-configurable granularity.                    |                                  |
27	| [`axi_burst_unwrap`](src/axi_burst_unwrap.sv)               | Convert AXI4 wrapping burst transfers into up to two incremental bursts.                             |                                  |
28	| [`axi_cdc`](src/axi_cdc.sv)                                 | AXI clock domain crossing based on a Gray FIFO implementation.                                       |                                  |
29	| [`axi_cut`](src/axi_cut.sv)                                 | Breaks all combinatorial paths between its input and output.                                         |                                  |
30	| [`axi_delayer`](src/axi_delayer.sv)                         | Synthesizable module which can (randomly) delays AXI channels.                                       |                                  |
31	| [`axi_demux_simple`](src/axi_demux_simple.sv)               | Demux without spill registers.                                                                       | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
32	| [`axi_demux`](src/axi_demux.sv)                             | Demultiplexes an AXI bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                               | [Doc](doc/axi_demux.md)          |
33	| [`axi_demux_id_counters`](src/axi_demux_id_counters.sv)     | ID counters used by `axi_demux` to track outstanding transactions.                                   |                                  |
34	| [`axi_dw_converter`](src/axi_dw_converter.sv)               | A data width converter between AXI interfaces of any data width.                                     |                                  |
35	| [`axi_dw_downsizer`](src/axi_dw_downsizer.sv)               | A data width converter between a wide AXI master and a narrower AXI slave.                           |                                  |
36	| [`axi_dw_upsizer`](src/axi_dw_upsizer.sv)                   | A data width converter between a narrow AXI master and a wider AXI slave.                            |                                  |
37	| [`axi_err_slv`](src/axi_err_slv.sv)                         | Always responds with an AXI decode/slave error for transactions which are sent to it.                |                                  |
38	| [`axi_fifo`](src/axi_fifo.sv)                               | A Fifo for each AXI4 channel to buffer requests.                                                     |                                  |
39	| [`axi_from_mem`](src/axi_from_mem.sv)                       | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4 requests downstream.                                    |                                  |
40	| [`axi_id_prepend`](src/axi_id_prepend.sv)                   | This module prepends/strips the MSB from the AXI IDs.                                                |                                  |
41	| [`axi_id_remap`](src/axi_id_remap.sv)                       | Remap AXI IDs from wide IDs at the slave port to narrower IDs at the master port.                    | [Doc][doc.axi_id_remap]          |
42	| [`axi_id_serialize`](src/axi_id_serialize.sv)               | Reduce AXI IDs by serializing transactions when necessary.                                           | [Doc][doc.axi_id_serialize]      |
43	| [`axi_interleaved_xbar`](src/axi_interleaved_xbar.sv)       | Interleaved version of the crossbar. This module is experimental; use at your own risk.              |                                  |
44	| [`axi_intf`](src/axi_intf.sv)                               | This file defines the interfaces we support.                                                         |                                  |
45	| [`axi_inval_filter`](src/axi_inval_filter.sv)               | Listens to AXI4 AW channel and issues single cacheline invalidations.                                |                                  |
46	| [`axi_isolate`](src/axi_isolate.sv)                         | A module that can isolate downstream slaves from receiving new AXI4 transactions.                    |                                  |
47	| [`axi_iw_converter`](src/axi_iw_converter.sv)               | Convert between any two AXI ID widths.                                                               | [Doc][doc.axi_iw_converter]      |
48	| [`axi_join`](src/axi_join.sv)                               | A connector that joins two AXI interfaces.                                                           |                                  |
49	| [`axi_lfsr`](src/axi_lfsr.sv)                               | AXI4-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.          |                                  |
50	| [`axi_lite_demux`](src/axi_lite_demux.sv)                   | Demultiplexes an AXI4-Lite bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.                         | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_demux.md)     |
51	| [`axi_lite_dw_converter`](src/axi_lite_dw_converter.sv)     | A data width converter between two AXI-Lite busses                                                   | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_dw_converter] |
52	| [`axi_lite_from_mem`](src/axi_lite_from_mem.sv)             | This module acts like an SRAM and makes AXI4-Lite requests downstream.                               |                                  |
53	| [`axi_lite_join`](src/axi_lite_join.sv)                     | A connector that joins two AXI-Lite interfaces.                                                      |                                  |
54	| [`axi_lite_lfsr`](src/axi_lite_lfsr.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite-attached LFSR; read returns pseudo-random data, writes are compressed into a checksum.     |                                  |
55	| [`axi_lite_mailbox`](src/axi_lite_mailbox.sv)               | A AXI4-Lite Mailbox with two slave ports and usage triggered irq.                                    | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mailbox.md)   |
56	| [`axi_lite_mux`](src/axi_lite_mux.sv)                       | Multiplexes AXI4-Lite slave ports down to one master port.                                           | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_mux.md)       |
57	| [`axi_lite_regs`](src/axi_lite_regs.sv)                     | AXI4-Lite registers with optional read-only and protection features.                                 | [Doc][doc.axi_lite_regs]         |
58	| [`axi_lite_to_apb`](src/axi_lite_to_apb.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to APB4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
59	| [`axi_lite_to_axi`](src/axi_lite_to_axi.sv)                 | AXI4-Lite to AXI4 protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
60	| [`axi_lite_xbar`](src/axi_lite_xbar.sv)                     | Fully-connected AXI4-Lite crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.               | [Doc](doc/axi_lite_xbar.md)      |
61	| [`axi_modify_address`](src/axi_modify_address.sv)           | A connector that allows addresses of AXI requests to be changed.                                     |                                  |
62	| [`axi_multicut`](src/axi_multicut.sv)                       | AXI register which can be used to relax timing pressure on long AXI buses.                           |                                  |
63	| [`axi_mux`](src/axi_mux.sv)                                 | Multiplexes the AXI4 slave ports down to one master port.                                            | [Doc](doc/axi_mux.md)            |
64	| [`axi_pkg`](src/axi_pkg.sv)                                 | Contains AXI definitions, common structs, and useful helper functions.                               |                                  |
65	| [`axi_rw_join`](src/axi_rw_join.sv)                         | Joins a read and a write slave into one single read / write master.                                  |                                  |
66	| [`axi_rw_split`](src/axi_rw_split.sv)                       | Splits a single read / write slave into one read and one write master.                               |                                  |
67	| [`axi_serializer`](src/axi_serializer.sv)                   | Serializes transactions with different IDs to the same ID.                                           |                                  |
68	| [`axi_throttle`](src/axi_throttle.sv)                       | Limits the maximum number of outstanding transfers sent to the downstream logic.                     |                                  |
69	| [`axi_test`](src/axi_test.sv)                               | A set of testbench utilities for AXI interfaces.                                                     |                                  |
70	| [`axi_to_axi_lite`](src/axi_to_axi_lite.sv)                 | AXI4 to AXI4-Lite protocol converter.                                                                |                                  |
71	| [`axi_to_mem`](src/axi_to_mem.sv)                           | AXI4 to memory protocol (req, gnt, rvalid) converter. Additional banked, interleaved, split variant. |                                  |
72	| [`axi_xbar`](src/axi_xbar.sv)                               | Fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports.               | [Doc](doc/axi_xbar.md)           |
73	| [`axi_xbar_unmuxed`](src/axi_xbar_unmuxed.sv)               | Demux side of fully-connected AXI4+ATOP crossbar with an arbitrary number of slave and master ports. | [Doc](doc/axi_xbar.md)           |
74	| [`axi_xp`](src/axi_xp.sv)                                   | AXI Crosspoint (XP) with homomorphous slave and master ports.                                        |                                  |
75	| [`axi_zero_mem`](src/axi_zero_mem.sv)                       | AXI-attached /dev/zero. All reads will be zero, writes are absorbed.                                 |                                  |
76	
77	## Synthesizable Verification Modules
78	
79	The following modules are meant to be used for verification purposes only but are synthesizable to be used in FPGA environments.
80	
81	| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                             |
82	|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| [`axi_bus_compare`](src/axi_bus_compare.sv)          | Compares two buses of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch.         |
84	| [`axi_fifo_delay_dyn`](src/axi_fifo_delay_dyn.sv)    | Delay and buffer each channel of an AXI bus individually.                                               |
85	| [`axi_slave_compare`](src/axi_slave_compare.sv)      | Compares two slave devices of the same type (and in the same clock domain), returns events on mismatch. |
86	
87	
88	### Simulation-Only Modules
89	
90	In addition to the modules above, which are available in synthesis and simulation, the following modules are available only in simulation.  Those modules are widely used in our testbenches, but they are also suitable to build testbenches for AXI modules and systems outside this repository.
91	
92	| Name                                                 | Description                                                                                            |
93	|------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
94	| [`axi_chan_compare`](src/axi_chan_compare.sv)        | Non-synthesizable module comparing two AXI channels of the same type                                   |
95	| [`axi_chan_logger`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Logs the transactions of an AXI4(+ATOPs) port to files.                                                |
96	| [`axi_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                      | Low-level driver for AXI4(+ATOPs) that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.           |
97	| [`axi_dumper`](src/axi_dumper.sv)                    | Dumps log to file to be interpreted by `axi_dumper_interpret` script for debugging purposes.           |
98	| [`axi_file_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4 master for file-based testbenches                                                                 |
99	| [`axi_lite_driver`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | Low-level driver for AXI4-Lite that can send and receive individual beats on any channel.              |
100	| [`axi_lite_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)            | AXI4-Lite master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.            |
101	| [`axi_lite_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)             | AXI4-Lite slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.     |
102	| [`axi_rand_master`](src/axi_test.sv)                 | AXI4(+ATOPs) master component that issues random transactions within user-defined constraints.         |
103	| [`axi_rand_slave`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | AXI4(+ATOPs) slave component that responds to transactions with constrainable random delays and data.  |
104	| [`axi_scoreboard`](src/axi_test.sv)                  | Scoreboard that models a memory that only gets changed by the monitored AXI4(+ATOPs) port.             |
105	| [`axi_sim_mem`](src/axi_sim_mem.sv)                  | Infinite memory with AXI4 slave port.                                                                  |
106	
107	
108	
109	## Atomic Operations
110	
111	AXI4+ATOPs means the full AXI4 specification plus atomic operations (ATOPs) as defined in Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec]. This has the following implications for modules that do not implement ATOPs and systems that include such modules:
112	
113	- Masters that do not issue ATOPs must set `aw_atop` to `'0`.
114	- Slaves that do not support ATOPs must specify this in their interface documentation and can ignore the `aw_atop` signal.
115	- System designers are responsible for ensuring that
116	  1. slaves that do not support ATOPs are behind an [`axi_atop_filter`](src/axi_atop_filter.sv) if any master could issue an ATOP to such slaves and
117	  2. the `aw_atop` signal is well-defined at the input of any (non-AXI4-Lite) module in this repository.
118	
119	Masters and slaves that do support ATOPs must adhere to Section E1.1 of the [AMBA 5 specification][AMBA 5 Spec].  In particular:
120	- ATOPs that have the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit set generate a write response (B channel) beat and at least one read response (R channel) beat.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their master port must be able to handle both B and R beats (in any order and without requiring a simultaneous handshake) for each such ATOP request.  All modules for which the `aw_atop[axi_pkg::ATOP_R_RESP]` bit could be set at their slave port must respond with the appropriate number of B and R beats for each such ATOP request.
121	- ATOPs must not use the same AXI ID as any other transaction that is outstanding at the same time.
122	
123	
124	## Which EDA Tools Are Supported?
125	
126	Our code is written in standard SystemVerilog ([IEEE 1800-2012][], to be precise), so the more important question is: Which subset of SystemVerilog does your EDA tool support?
127	
128	We aim to be compatible with a wide range of EDA tools.  For this reason, we strive to use as simple language constructs as possible, especially for our synthesizable modules.  We encourage contributions that further simplify our code to make it compatible with even more EDA tools.  We also welcome contributions that work around problems that specific EDA tools may have with our code, as long as:
129	- the EDA tool is reasonably widely used,
130	- recent versions of the EDA tool are affected,
131	- the workaround does not break functionality in other tools, and
132	- the workaround does not significantly complicate code or add maintenance overhead.
133	
134	In addition, we suggest to report issues with the SystemVerilog language support directly to the EDA vendor. Our code is fully open and
135	can / should be shared with the EDA vendor as a testcase for any language problem encountered.
136	
137	All code in each release and on the default branch is tested on a recent version of at least one industry-standard RTL simulator and synthesizer.  You can examine the [CI settings](./.gitlab-ci.yml) to find out which version of which tool we are running.
138	
139	
140	[AMBA 5 Spec]: https://developer.arm.com/documentation/ihi0022/hc
141	[IEEE 1800-2012]: https://standards.ieee.org/standard/1800-2012.html
142	[doc.axi_id_remap]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_remap
143	[doc.axi_id_serialize]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_id_serialize
144	[doc.axi_iw_converter]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_iw_converter
145	[doc.axi_lite_regs]: https://pulp-platform.github.io/axi/master/module.axi_lite_regs
146
